element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns basic destruction mechanics without enemy pressure
Players learn spatial navigation and consequences of destruction order
Players discover combo scoring and multi-object destruction strategies
Enemies that disrupt gameplay by knocking objects and causing falls; avoidance becomes core mechanic
Projectile threats that require spatial awareness beyond enemy collision
Tactical tool to neutralize enemies by redirecting them off-screen
Optional scoring mechanics that reward optimal destruction sequences
Environmental obstacles that cannot be destroyed; demand route-planning around fixed hazards

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully destroying all required objects in a level
Opening doors to send enemies harmlessly away
typical_tension_triggers: Running out of safe destruction options
Risk of becoming trapped in barrel (level abort required)
Making wrong destruction choice that blocks level completion
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Puzzle-driven pacing with moment-to-moment tension from enemy threat and spatial constraints. Relief cycles when threats are avoided and objectives progress. Competitive variant in '98 version increases constant pressure.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Bonus items appear conditionally based on destruction sequences

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of basic destruction mechanics without heavy enemy pressure; player learns that destruction order matters (irreversible consequences for unreachable objects)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of bombs, fireballs, and more complex enemy patterns; spatial constraint complexity increases; multi-objective puzzle solving required
notable_spikes: First appearance of fireballs (new threat type)
Levels requiring precise destruction sequencing to avoid self-trapping
Late-game stages combining multiple threat types simultaneously

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level is an invisible grid where player must destroy specific object sets. No jumping allowed (hammer weight mechanic). Success requires planning optimal destruction order to avoid blocking own path.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Generic level structure applies across all 100 original levels

notable_level_patterns.description: Spike and other enemies actively disrupt by knocking objects and causing falls. Doors can redirect threats. Multi-layer puzzle solving: navigate grid destruction AND avoid enemies.
Destroying multiple connected objects in sequence (especially via bombs) grants bonus points. Encourages players to plan destructions that create cascades.
Unlike original's objective-based clearing, '98 version shifts to competitive mechanics with block-matching gameplay; different pacing and tension model
